evals.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS company building a project management tool for construction firms. Our Q3 roadmap has 9 features: (1) Gantt chart view, (2) subcontractor portal, (3) photo documentation with GPS tagging, (4) invoice approval workflow, (5) material cost tracking, (6) weather integration, (7) daily site reports, (8) equipment checkout log, (9) dark mode. We have 6 engineers and 14 weeks. Support tickets are dominated by 'where are my photos from last week' and 'I can't see what my subs are doing'. Churn interviews say the #1 reason accounts leave is they go back to spreadsheets because 'your reporting takes too long'. What should we ship?
evals.expected_output: CPA runs all 7 blocks. Spots identified from highest-signal items — photo documentation and subcontractor visibility should survive given ticket data. Daily reports likely in bundle with photo docs. Dark mode should be void. Novel finding should challenge or validate the Gantt chart priority. Danger zone likely on invoice workflow (high complexity). SHIP/HOLD/KILL clearly separates the 9 features.

assertions.text: Identifies at least one feature as a BUNDLE that must ship together
Identifies dark mode or weather integration as VOID or KILL
Uses churn interview data to calibrate k parameter
Produces a SHIP/HOLD/KILL report with explicit decisions for all 9 features
Novel finding passes template-swap test — is not generic advice that applies to any SaaS

evals.prompt: I'm running a consumer fintech app — we help gig workers track income and estimate quarterly taxes. We launched 8 months ago, 12k MAU, 34% week-4 retention. We're debating whether to add: (A) bank account connection via Plaid, (B) automatic mileage tracking, (C) receipt scanning, (D) multi-state tax support, (E) Stripe/Venmo income import. Users are mostly Uber/DoorDash drivers and Etsy sellers. Our NPS comments say 'I still have to manually enter everything' 47 times in the last 3 months. We're 4 people. What do we actually build next?
evals.expected_output: Field should identify bank/income import as likely bundle (both address the manual entry complaint). Receipt scanning may be a phantom dependent on camera infrastructure. Multi-state tax support is probably a tangle — underspecified. Mileage tracking competes with bank connection for same user attention (demand overlap). SHIP/HOLD/KILL surfaces the phantom chain issue as hidden roadmap.

assertions.text: Identifies phantom chain or hidden dependency in the feature set
Uses the 47 NPS comment pattern as f-parameter calibration evidence
Multi-state tax support flagged as tangle, labyrinth, or architecture review
Demand overlap identified between at least two features competing for same user job
Report accounts for the 4-person team size as a resource constraint

evals.prompt: We already shipped 6 features last quarter and I'm worried we made the wrong calls. We built: native video playback, comment threads, advanced search filters, user profile customization, email digest, and an API. Engagement on video and comments is strong — 68% of sessions touch both. Search filters: 22% usage, mostly power users. Profile customization: 8% usage, no correlation with retention. Email digest: opened by 31% but click-through is 2.1%. API: 3 enterprise customers use it, these are our highest-paying accounts. Run a pressure test on what we already shipped to tell me if we made good calls.
evals.expected_output: Retrospective CPA run. Video + comments should emerge as a real bundle — validated by the 68% co-session data. Profile customization flags as probable shortcut survivor or false demand — 8% usage, no retention signal. Email digest is a danger zone — high open rate masks near-zero engagement. Search filters are a genuine spot but power-user only (low Du). API is a structural differentiator — small user base, high value concentration.

assertions.text: Video + comments identified as bundle based on co-session behavioral data
Profile customization flagged as SHORTCUT SURVIVAL or false demand
Email digest flagged as DANGER ZONE — high open rate misleading signal
API classified as STRUCTURAL DIFFERENTIATOR based on revenue concentration
Runs all blocks including Block 7 verification checks

evals.prompt: Product team is arguing. Designer wants to rebuild the onboarding flow — says it's 'outdated and confusing'. Engineering wants to pay down auth debt — the login system is held together with string and SSH prayers. Sales says we need SSO yesterday or we keep losing enterprise deals. Marketing wants a new landing page with better positioning. CEO says just ship more features. Support says the top 3 tickets are all about the same CSV import bug that's been open 6 months. We have 8 weeks and 5 people. Help.
evals.expected_output: CSV import bug is a keystone constraint — it's bleeding u(x) from multiple features by tainting perception. Auth debt is a KEYSTONE architectural constraint with cascade unlock. SSO is a genuine spot but requires auth debt resolution (phantom prerequisite). Onboarding is probably a constraint void — blocked by unclear ICP, not UX. Landing page is political. SHIP/HOLD/KILL cuts clearly through the argument with structural reasoning.

assertions.text: CSV import bug identified as keystone constraint, not a feature
SSO identified as phantom dependent on auth debt resolution
At least one item identified as POLITICAL constraint, not product decision
Novel finding addresses the team alignment subtext, not just the feature list
Produces actionable SHIP/HOLD/KILL decisions for each of the 6 competing demands

evals.prompt: We're about to start Q4 planning. Before we write anything down, I want to know what gaps exist in our current product that we're probably not seeing. We make a CRM for independent insurance agents. Current features: contact management, pipeline tracking, policy renewal reminders, commission reporting, document storage, email integration. Our top 3 competitors also have all of these. Users say we're 'easier to use' but that's not a growth moat. What are we missing?
evals.expected_output: Gap-finding CPA. Existing features all table-stakes (template-swap fails for all — any competitor has them). Phantom chains should surface: referral tracking (agents live on referrals, no feature exists), compliance documentation (E&O audit trail), client household view (insurance is family-unit not individual). Latent demand analysis relevant — agents may not articulate these needs but behavioral patterns suggest them. Novel finding should identify the structural differentiator opportunity, not just a feature list.

assertions.text: All existing features correctly classified as TABLE STAKES via competitor swap test
At least one LATENT demand feature identified with behavioral reasoning
At least one PHANTOM PREREQUISITE or gap identified that no current competitor has clearly solved
Novel finding is specific to insurance agents, not generic CRM advice
Structural differentiator opportunity identified — something that would be hard to replicate
